# Chapter 3: User research and human-centred design  (~85 pp)
- 3.1 What user research is and what it is not (10) · 3.2 Methods: interviews, contextual inquiry, ethnography (15) · 3.3 Surveys, A/B testing, analytics, telemetry (10) · 3.4 Personas, journey maps, jobs-to-be-done (8) · 3.5 Accessibility and inclusive design as research practice (10) · 3.6 Synthesis: insights to requirements (10) · 3.7 Failure section: research that asked the wrong questions; the wrong-user trap (12) · 3.8 Worked examples: medical-device user research, industrial-control-room redesign (10)

**Archetype**: interface, ethics, uncertainty. **Project**: conduct user research for one product the reader uses; produce five actionable insights. **Exercises**: 18.
